events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: **needs_more_info**, high risk. The closeout description uses generic checklist language ("labels, supports, terminations") that doesn't correspond to actual OSHA 1926 Subpart P trench-protection criteria — there's no confirmation of soil classification, protective-system sizing, competent-person daily inspections, egress ladder spacing, or spoil-pile setback. Given trench collapse is a high-severity hazard, a clean generic inspection card isn't sufficient evidence of compliance without those specific records.
